title: The nonlinear stability of the Schwarzschild metric without symmetry
speaker: Mihalis Dafermos
abstract: I will discuss an upcoming result proving the full finite-codimension non-linear asymptotic stability of the Schwarzschild family as solutions to the Einstein vacuum equations in the exterior of the black hole region. No symmetry is assumed. The work is based on our previous understanding of linear stability of Schwarzschild in double null gauge. Joint work with G. Holzegel, I. Rodnianski and M. Taylor.

proof_stages.label: Schwarzschild exterior as a dynamical stability problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk introduces the Schwarzschild family, its modern black-hole and two-ended interpretation, the Cauchy-evolution viewpoint for Einstein vacuum, and the reduction of the perturbation problem to characteristic data on two null cones in the exterior.

proof_stages.label: Nonlinear theorem with Kerr obstruction and codimension-three restriction
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem is formulated as asymptotic stability of Schwarzschild in the black-hole exterior for the codimension-three class of perturbations that do not radiate toward Kerr, including complete future null infinity, a smooth event horizon, orbital closeness, and convergence to a Schwarzschild metric.

proof_stages.label: Nonlinear-compatible formulation of linear stability
proof_stages.summary: Linear stability is defined in the form needed for nonlinear work—choose a well-posed gauge, linearize, prove boundedness and sufficiently fast decay modulo residual gauge—and the relevant double-null-gauge theorem is stated, with decay to a standard linearized Kerr solution.

proof_stages.label: Gauge-invariant Teukolsky control via Regge–Wheeler transformation
proof_stages.summary: The proof first controls the gauge-invariant curvature components through the Teukolsky equation, scalar-wave black-hole estimates and mode-stability input, and then uses Chandrasekhar-type physical-space transformations to a good Regge–Wheeler equation to obtain decay and invert back.

proof_stages.label: Hierarchical reconstruction of the full linearized metric
proof_stages.summary: After the gauge-invariant quantities are estimated, the linearized double-null structure and Bianchi equations are solved hierarchically to recover all metric variables, adding a residual pure gauge solution where necessary and isolating the codimension-three data that decay to linearized Schwarzschild.

proof_stages.label: Nonlinear null structure in double-null gauge
proof_stages.summary: The nonlinear argument uses double-null gauge because it exposes the Einstein null condition and produces good quadratic terms, including in the nonlinear analogue of the Regge–Wheeler equation for the transformed quantity.

proof_stages.label: Teleological gauge choice and nonlinear selection of the Schwarzschild-attracting data
proof_stages.summary: The nonlinear closure requires future-selected double-null normalizations, a teleological determination of the event horizon, and a bootstrap in which the codimension-three Schwarzschild-attracting set is determined only in the limit rather than directly from the initial data.

proof_stages.label: Outlook beyond Schwarzschild
proof_stages.summary: The talk concludes that full Kerr asymptotic stability should have no new conceptual obstruction beyond the mechanisms discussed, while cosmological-constant variants—especially Kerr–anti-de Sitter—form a major future frontier.

examples.name: Light-signal test in the Schwarzschild Penrose diagram
examples.note: Specific events in the diagram are tested by whether their outgoing light signals reach future null infinity, making the black-hole region concrete without contributing to the proof.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker uses the Schwarzschild Penrose diagram to trace which events can send light signals to null infinity and thereby identifies the black-hole region

examples.name: Axisymmetric zero-angular-momentum perturbations
examples.note: These data cannot radiate angular momentum and hence must asymptote to Schwarzschild, illustrating a recognizable subfamily of the teleologically defined codimension-three set.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker applies the stability statement to axisymmetric zero-angular-momentum data, reasoning that angular momentum cannot be radiated and hence the limit must be Schwarzschild

passes.reason: Axisymmetry and vanishing angular momentum impose symmetry and collapse the rotational Kerr charge.
